en.extract:
The Principe scops owl (Otus bikegila) is a species of scops owl found only on Príncipe Island of São Tomé and Príncipe, an island country in the Gulf of Guinea off the coast of Africa. First noticed by its distinctive nighttime call, it was formally described in 2022. The first records of suspicions of its existence are from 1928. Given its low population numbers and tiny range, researchers have asked the IUCN to declare it Critically Endangered, and this status was granted in 2023. Its distribution is limited to native forests where human activity is low—in fact, its range is entirely within a protected area, the Príncipe Obô Natural Park—and it seems to prefer larger trees. It feeds on insects. Its predators include the Mona monkey and the black rat.


Call
The bird's call has been described as "tuu," repeated quickly, sometimes in duets. The birds begin to call almost as soon as it gets dark. Another of its calls is "cat-like" and can exist either in isolation or repetition. Duets are common; females call at a higher pitch.


Etymology
The owl's Latin name was given in honor of park ranger and former parrot harvester Ceciliano "Bikegila" do Bom Jesus.


See also
List of bird species described in the 2020s


References

The '''Principe scops owl''' ('''''Otus bikegila''''') is a species of [[scops owl]] found only on [[Príncipe Island]] of [[São Tomé and Príncipe]], an island country in the [[Gulf of Guinea]] off the coast of [[Africa]]. First noticed by its distinctive nighttime call, it was formally described in 2022.<ref>{{cite journal |title=A new species of scops-owl (Aves, Strigiformes, Strigidae, ''Otus'') from Príncipe Island (Gulf of Guinea, Africa) and novel insights into the systematic affinities within ''Otus'' |year=2022 |last1=Melo |first1=Martim |last2=Freitas |first2=Bárbara |last3=Verbelen |first3=Philippe |doi=10.31219/osf.io/mfubj }}</ref> Given its low population numbers and tiny range, researchers have asked the [[International Union for Conservation of Nature|IUCN]] to declare it [[Critically |last1=Freitas |first1=Bárbara |last2=Melo |first2=Martim |last3=do Bom Jesus |first3=Ceciliano |last4=da Costa |first4=Sátiro R. |last5=dos Santos |first5=Yodiney |last6=Crottini |first6=Angelica entirely within a protected area, the Príncipe Obô Natural Park<ref name=":0" />—and it seems to prefer larger trees.<ref name=":1" /> It feeds on insects.<ref name=":1" /> Its predators include the [[Mona monkey]] and the [[black rat]].<ref name=":1" />

==Call==

The bird's call has been described as "tuu," repeated quickly, sometimes in duets. The birds begin to call almost as soon as it gets dark.<ref name=Eureka>{{cite press release |url=https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/969506 |author=Pensoft Publishers |title=New species of owl discovered in the rainforests of Príncipe Island, Central Africa |date=October 31, 2022 |access-date=November 1, 2022}}</ref> Another of its calls is "cat-like" and can exist either in isolation or repetition.<ref name=":0" /> Duets are common; females call at a higher pitch.<ref name=":0" />

==Etymology==
The owl's Latin name was given in honor of park ranger and former parrot harvester Ceciliano "Bikegila" do Bom Jesus.<ref name=Eureka />

zh.extract:
普林西比角鸮（學名：Otus bikegila），英語：Principe Scops Owl，是鴞形目鴟鴞科角鴞屬下的一種鳥類。該物種僅分布於普林西比島上，是該島第八個被發現的特有鳥類。目前因為其極其有限的分布範圍及族群數量，因此在保護現狀中被評為極危物種。

本物種為經由其與已知角鴞不同的叫聲，於2016年所發現的物種。研究人員根據其形態、羽毛顏色和花紋、叫聲和分子證據於2022年時描述。根據當地人的說法，本物種最早可能在1928年時就已有所紀錄。屬名Otus來自拉丁語的同名詞，指短耳鴞，此詞源自希臘語的ὦτος（羅馬化：ōtos），意為「有耳朵的貓頭鷹」。種小名bikegila則來自當地一位原為鸚鵡獵人，現為自然嚮導的當地居民Ceciliano do Bom Jesus，其外號為Bikegila，研究人員根據其證言發現了這種角鴞。
作為一種角鴞，普林西比角鴞的外觀接近同樣分布於幾內亞灣的島嶼上的聖多美角鴞，但黑斑更小、更細。這個物種有兩種顏色型態，一種深栗色，另一種為上半身呈栗色，臉部和下半身大多為灰色的型態，雌鳥比雄鳥略大。其耳羽僅在被驚嚇時才會抬起，因此平時多為圓形的頭部。
其叫聲類似於昆蟲叫聲，多在日落後可聽見「tuu」的叫聲，每秒約1個音符的快速速度重複，用以保衛領土並吸引伴侶。另一個被記錄到的叫聲則類似貓。雌雄的二重唱在島上相當常見；其中雌鳥的叫聲較高。
普林西比角鴞的分佈範圍僅侷限於普林西比島上僅34.4平方公里大的聖多美奧博自然公園的原生森林內，且該地有一部分區域正有開發為小型發電水壩的計畫。其生存環境就如同當地的另一特有種普林西比鸫一樣倍受入侵種白腹長尾猴的威脅。目前其族群預測僅有約1000—1500隻左右，因此在《國際自然保護聯盟瀕危物種紅色名錄》中這個物種被列為極危物種。
